### Post by Ubuntiac on 2007-09-07
OK,

This isn't a fix for how to get S-video working, but if you'd just like to get your desktop back, you can revert to the more stable (yet without sexy advanced features) open source driver by typing:

sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf

and replacing:
Driver "nvidia"

With:
Driver "nv"

Then just press Crtl X to save and accept. This will disable the Nvidia driver, but get your desktop working enough to hopefully figure out what's up. Posting the results of /var/log/Xorg.0.log (before restarting) would help someone smarter than me as well, paying special attention to any lines starting with EE (which means error) and say anything about nvidia.

The good news is that the final Gutsy relase should have a feature called "Bulletproof X" so that in future something like this would happen automatically, leaving you with a working desktop instead of a blank screen.

Good luck and let us know what you find!

---

### Post by HybridTheory on 2007-09-07
Sorry it took so long for me to get back alright so the nano thing does bring up what you said (something i didnt know about thanks) but i dont see my xorg text any where its just a black screen where i can type with alot of stuff under it like ^X Exit and such :(

### Post by dwhitney67 on 2007-09-07
Would it be possible for you to edit your /etc/X11/xorg.conf file to restore it to it's working version before you added the S-Video stuff??

Once you boot up, you should at least be able to get a to a console.  X11 is one of the last things that are started when a system comes up, therefore to get to a login console you could always type ctrl - alt - F2.

Then just login and do you editing via the command line.

---
